---
title: "jaxcmr"
description: "JAX-accelerated toolkit for computational memory research"
---

jaxcmr is a Python library for specifying and evaluating
computational models of memory search. It leverages a [compositional
design](https://refactoring.guru/design-patterns/bridge) and
[JAX](https://docs.jax.dev/en/latest/#)'s support for easy just-in-time
compilation to streamline model prototyping and simulation so
researchers can explore more ideas in less time.


## Installation

```bash
pip install jaxcmr
```

Or install the development version:

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/githubpsyche/jaxcmr
```

## Explore

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Get Started](docs_src/get-started/)
Installation and quickstart tutorials.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Concepts](docs_src/concepts/)
Library architecture, JAX fundamentals, and core abstractions.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Analyses](docs_src/analyses/)
Behavioral measures for recall data.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Models](docs_src/models/)
CMR and memory search model variants.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Evaluation](docs_src/evaluation/)
Fitting, simulation, and model comparison.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Workflow](docs_src/workflow/)
Research practice, templates, and tooling.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Projects](projects/)
Research manuscripts using jaxcmr.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Blog](docs_src/blog/)
Updates and research notes.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [GitHub](https://github.com/githubpsyche/jaxcmr)
Source code and issue tracker.
:::

:::
